nodes.text: Hi. Sertraline, fifty milligrams, Becca Stinson. Prescription should be called in already.

responses.text: Let me check. I'll need your ID.

nodes.text: Here. It's a hospital badge, not a driver's license — is that okay? No, wait, I have the actual ID. Sorry, force of habit.

responses.text: State ID is fine. Thank you.

nodes.text: Twelve hours. Two admits, one discharge, and a code in the afternoon. I'm fine, I just want to go home.

responses.text: I'll get you out of here fast. ID?
You work at the hospital down the street?

nodes.text: Three blocks east. Seven years on the floor. I know how this part works — you're going to scan my ID, verify my date of birth, run the insurance, and I'll have it in ten minutes. I'm not in a rush. I just want to be done.

responses.text: You do know how it works. ID when you're ready.

nodes.text: The prescription is from Dr. Okafor. Helen Okafor. Psychiatry. NPI is on the script. It's all legitimate — I know how it looks, an RN filling a psych prescription, but I just want to be clear that it's normal.

responses.text: I didn't assume otherwise. Let me run this through.
Sertraline from psychiatry is completely routine.

nodes.text: I know. I just say it so we don't have to do the dance.
